commit.message:
Fixes #4789 - Makes menu editing easier + Application cleanup (#4790)

* Add View.AddAt() and improve focus/menu item handling

Adds View.AddAt(int, View) for indexed subview insertion and refactors Add(View) to use it. Refactors focus management by replacing _previouslyFocused with a public PreviouslyFocused property and updates all related logic. Updates the Menus scenario to allow dynamic adding/removing of MenuItems using new UI buttons and AddAt(). Improves Menu.SelectedMenuItem to fall back to PreviouslyFocused when not focused. Includes minor code cleanups, XML doc improvements, and better handling of focus navigation when no subviews are focusable.

* Add AddAt() unit tests and refactor test initializers

Refactor View and Size/Thickness initializations in SubviewTests.cs to use concise object initializers and explicit types. Add a comprehensive suite of unit tests for the new AddAt() method, covering insertion at various indices, null and out-of-range handling, event raising, cancellation, and order preservation. Improve test readability and maintainability. No production code changes.

* Update Terminal.Gui/ViewBase/View.Navigation.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update Terminal.Gui/ViewBase/View.cs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add tests for PreviouslyFocused property in View

Added PreviouslyFocusedTests to cover focus navigation logic in
the View class. Tests include default state, tracking and
clearing of PreviouslyFocused, restoring focus, handling of
non-focusable and removed views, and nested view hierarchies.
Ensures PreviouslyFocused behaves correctly in all scenarios.

* Add InlineMenuBarItem as proof-of-concept for non-modal inline menus

Introduces InlineMenuBarItem and IMenuBarEntry interface to prove that
MenuBar can support both popover (modal) and inline (non-modal) menu
entries. This is a stepping stone toward adding a UsePopoverMenu
behavior switch on MenuBarItem itself.

Key changes:
- IMenuBarEntry: Interface abstraction for menu bar entries
- InlineMenuBarItem: MenuItem-derived entry using inline SubMenu
- MenuBar: Generalized to work with IMenuBarEntry (not just MenuBarItem)
- MenuBar: Added ShowEntry close-other-entries, FocusInlineSubMenuIfNeeded,
  IsInlineSubMenu guard, IsSwitchingItem property
- Fixed reentrant toggle-close bug via IsSwitchingItem guard
- SubMenu key forwarding (Left/Right/Escape) to parent MenuBar
- 25 unit tests + 3 integration tests, all passing
- Full test suite (14,516 tests) passes with no regressions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fold InlineMenuBarItem into MenuBarItem via UsePopoverMenu property

Replace the separate InlineMenuBarItem class with an init-only
UsePopoverMenu property on MenuBarItem. When true (default), MenuBarItem
uses a modal PopoverMenu. When false, it uses the inherited SubMenu
mechanism for non-modal inline menus.

- Add UsePopoverMenu init-only property (default true) to MenuBarItem
- Route IMenuBarEntry implementation based on UsePopoverMenu
- Port inline toggle-close guard and SubMenu key forwarding from
  InlineMenuBarItem into MenuBarItem
- Update MenuBar to dispatch on UsePopoverMenu instead of concrete type
- Delete InlineMenuBarItem.cs
- Update all unit and integration tests
- Add UsePopoverMenu toggle checkbox to MenuBars scenario

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix navigation asserts when toggling UsePopoverMenu checkbox

Close open menus, move focus away, and remove orphaned inline SubMenus
from the SuperView before disposing the old MenuBar during rebuild.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add InlineMenus scenario and fix cascading SubMenu disposal bug

- Create dedicated InlineMenus scenario with cascading submenus
  demonstrating UsePopoverMenu = false (all-inline and mixed modes)
- Revert MenuBars scenario to original static bottom MenuBar
- Fix cascading SubMenu disposal: PopoverMenu.OnSubViewAdded adds
  cascading SubMenu Menus as direct SubViews of PopoverMenu, so
  View.Dispose would destroy their contents during EndInit conversion.
  Fix: call RemoveAll before Dispose to detach all SubViews safely.
- Add cascading SubMenu tests covering 2-level, 3-level, navigation,
  escape, and mixed-mode scenarios

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor MenuBar activation, IDs, and debug support

- Split design-time menu logic into EnableForDesign<TContext>
- Improved debug IDs for menus, submenus, and popovers
- Refactored activation, entry finding, and submenu focus logic
- Ensured proper SubMenu disposal and initialization checks
- Fixed MenuItem.ValueChanged event handler bug
- Updated InlineMenus demo and added IDs for easier debugging
- Changed View.ToString/ToDebugString to use ToIdentifyingString
- Updated tests to match new ToString output and anchor checks
- General code cleanup and improved code organization

* Refactor inline menu scenario/tests; add popover mouse test

Renamed InlineMenus to MenuBarsWithoutPopovers for clarity. Replaced InlineMenuBarItemTests with MenuBarItemWithoutPopoverTests, preserving and improving coverage for MenuBarItem.UsePopoverMenu = false. Added MouseGrabbingView and a new test to ensure popover is not dismissed when a subview grabs the mouse and is dragged outside. Minor code cleanups in popover mouse tests. Improves clarity and robustness of inline menu and popover behaviors.

* Improve popover mouse dismissal logic and tests

Popover dismissal now checks for mouse grabs within the popover hierarchy, preventing unwanted dismissal during drag operations. Added IsGrabbedByViewInHierarchy helper. DropDownList popover always enables vertical scrollbar. Refactored and expanded popover mouse dismissal tests for clarity and coverage, including a new test for drag scenarios.

* Rename KeyboardImpl/MouseImpl to ApplicationKeyboard/Mouse

Replaces KeyboardImpl and MouseImpl with ApplicationKeyboard and ApplicationMouse throughout the codebase, including all references in code, tests, and documentation. Implementation files have been renamed accordingly, and a new ApplicationKeyboardThreadSafetyTests.cs replaces the old thread safety tests. No functional changes—this is a naming and organizational update for clarity and consistency.

* Refactor Application static API; rename Popover to Popovers

- Replace Application.Popover with Application.Popovers throughout code and tests
- Refactor static Application API into partial classes, delegating to instance-based ApplicationImpl
- Mark all legacy static APIs as obsolete; encourage instance-based usage
- Move key config properties (QuitKey, ArrangeKey, etc.) to Application.cs with change events
- Reintroduce and document IPopover, IPopoverView, PopoverImpl, and Popover<TView, TResult>
- Update all popover-related docs and code to use Application.Popovers
- Update tests to use Application.Popovers
- Clean up Application class summary, SupportedCultures logic, and add "Referes" to dictionary

* Switch test logging to BindTo with LogLevel.Warning

Replaced TestLogging.Verbose with TestLogging.BindTo(LogLevel.Warning) in multiple test files to reduce log verbosity. Added necessary using directives for logging and test output integration. This standardizes and streamlines logging across the test suite.

* Guard BridgedCancellation trace asserts with #if DEBUG

BridgedCancellation trace warning asserts in ViewCommandTests.cs are now only included in DEBUG builds to avoid affecting release builds. Updated .nupkg and .snupkg files as part of the build process. No changes to test logic.

* Refactor Arranger to use dedicated ArrangerButton class

Introduce ArrangerButton, a specialized Button subclass for border arrangement controls, replacing generic Button usage in Arranger. This encapsulates arrangement type via a ButtonType property and auto-selects glyphs, eliminating the need for external mapping. Updates all related code and tests to use ArrangerButton, improving type safety and maintainability. Also sets Application.MaximumIterationsPerSecond default to 25 and performs minor test cleanups.

* Update source index and docs for accuracy and clarity

- Refactored INDEX.md to reference actual source files per agent/component, improving retrieval and navigation.
- Added new/renamed files to the index; moved legacy files to App/Legacy.
- Reorganized and clarified views.md: improved control descriptions, updated ASCII art, added sections for type-safe generics, and refreshed examples for TableView and Wizard.
- Fixed cref references and namespace for ICommandContext; clarified command routing docs.
- Simplified CharMap remarks.
- Updated AGENTS.md to point to .claude/cookbook/ and emphasize retrieval-led reasoning.
- Improves maintainability and usability of both codebase index and documentation.

---------

* Run results collection step with Bash

* fix: guard inputs.skip-comment in issue_comment workflows and update docs

- Replace `inputs.skip-comment` with `github.event_name == 'workflow_call' && inputs.skip-comment || 'false'` in all 7 fix workflows so that issue_comment triggers don't access the unavailable inputs context
- Remove redundant `&& inputs.skip-comment != 'true'` from reaction step conditions that already gate on `github.event_name == 'issue_comment'`
- Update format-all.yaml patch message to include artifact name and apply instructions

Co-authored-by: greenc-FNAL <2372949+greenc-FNAL@users.noreply.github.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ref descriptions, docs examples, eyes reaction, patch artifact message

Co-authored-by: greenc-FNAL <2372949+greenc-FNAL@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address remaining Copilot review comments

- Per https://github.com/Framework-R-D/phlex/pull/385#pullrequestreview-3889460827

* Address latest review

- Per https://github.com/Framework-R-D/phlex/pull/385#pullrequestreview-3890926156

* Harden Remove eyes reaction step in all fix workflows

Wrap listForIssueComment call in try/catch so a transient API error does
not fail the workflow. Narrow the bot-match predicate from the broad
r.user.type === 'Bot' to r.user && r.user.login === 'github-actions[bot]'
so only the reaction posted by phlexbot's pre-check step is removed.

Use destructured { data: reactions } assignment for consistency with
format-all.yaml.

* Declare on.workflow_call.outputs in all fix workflows

format-all.yaml reads changes/pushed/commit_sha_short/patch_name from
needs.<job>.outputs.* for each called fix workflow. GitHub Actions only
surfaces these to the caller when they are explicitly re-exported under
on.workflow_call.outputs; job-level outputs alone are not visible to
calling workflows.

Add on.workflow_call.outputs sections to all seven fix workflows,
mapping to the corresponding apply-job outputs.

* Guard PR lookup in handle-fix-commit against missing issue context

The Get PR maintainer_can_modify property step uses context.issue.number,
which is only populated when the root trigger is an issue_comment event.
When a fix workflow is triggered via workflow_dispatch, context.issue.number
is undefined, causing the pulls.get API call to fail whenever formatting
changes are found.

Note: workflow_call from format-all.yaml (triggered by issue_comment) is
not affected — GitHub propagates the event payload to called workflows so
context.issue.number is populated there. The bug is specific to the
workflow_dispatch path.

Default to 'false' for maintainer_can_modify when no PR context is
available. The commit path's first condition (same-repo check) still
allows pushes to proceed correctly for workflow_dispatch runs.

commit.message:
feat: multi-airway chaining for complete route coverage

Replace single-airway follow with a chaining loop (max 8 airways):
- After each airway ends, find the next heading-aligned airway from
  the last waypoint and continue following toward the destination
- Track used (name, fragment) pairs to prevent infinite cycles
- Update heading between chains using bearing toward destination
- AirwayPathResult now carries IReadOnlyList<string> AirwaysUsed

For EHRD→LEBL this produces ~29 points via UY131→Z319→UL194→UN860
instead of the previous 4 points from a single airway fragment.

Nav data log now shows the full airway chain and clarifies that
the ARINC file only contains 4 NL terminal-area fixes (not applicable
for en-route plotting).

refactor: sun arc ellipse shifts based on daylight ratio

The ellipse now uses a single uniform vertical radius and shifts
vertically relative to the horizon based on the daylight fraction:
- Equinox (12h/12h): ellipse centered on horizon
- Summer (more daylight): ellipse shifts up, more above horizon
- Winter (less daylight): ellipse shifts down, more below horizon

Offset formula: arcRadiusY * (2 * dayFraction - 1)

Also moved the 'Sunrise & Sunset' title inside the canvas, centered
just above the horizon line, so the ellipse encircles it.

perf: cache de arquivos estáticos em RAM + defer no brasao_b64.js

Problema: todos os arquivos estáticos (HTML, JS, CSS, PNG) eram lidos
do disco pelo Flask a cada requisição, passando pelo interceptor do
OneDrive que adicionava latência em cada open() de arquivo.

Solução:
- server.py: _preload_static_files() carrega TODOS os arquivos para
  um dict em RAM no startup (~2000KB, ~50 arquivos)
- Rotas /, /static/<f>, /<f>: servem do _file_cache dict sem tocar
  o disco, com fallback para send_from_directory se não encontrar
- index.html, fornecimento.html: brasao_b64.js (436KB) agora com
  'defer' — não bloqueia o render da página enquanto carrega
- index.html: app.js também com 'defer' para download paralelo

Resultado:
- brasao_b64.js: 2000ms → 9ms (222x mais rápido)
- index.html: ~500ms+ → 88ms primeira, 7ms subsequentes
- Todos os estáticos: 3-11ms (antes: centenas de ms via OneDrive)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: sun arc uses location timezone and shows night position

The sun arc now computes the current time in the viewed location's
timezone (via Open-Meteo's timezone field) instead of using the
device's local clock. This fixes incorrect sun positions when viewing
locations in different timezones.

The visual is redesigned with a full day/night elliptical path:
- Upper arc (golden) = daytime, sunrise to sunset
- Lower arc (blue-grey) = nighttime, sunset to sunrise
- Sun dot travels the complete 24-hour path, going below the
  horizon after sunset

Fixes #2

commit.message:
Prevent co-expression OOM on large studies (#12005)

* Prevent co-expression OOM on large studies with external group by

The rankCorr() aggregate function maintains one accumulator state per
gene group (~20K simultaneous states), each holding all sample pairs.
For large studies like pancan_pdmr_2025 (6,273 samples × 20K genes),
this exceeds available memory and crashes ClickHouse.

Add SETTINGS max_bytes_before_external_group_by = 2000000000 to spill
aggregation state to disk when it exceeds 2GB. This prevents OOM while
adding minimal overhead for smaller studies (~1.2x slowdown vs 1x).

Tested across multiple study sizes:
- chol_tcga (37 samples): 0.8s → 0.8s (no change)
- brca_tcga (1,101 samples): 15s → 19s (1.2x)
- pancan_pdmr_2025 (6,273 samples): OOM → 85s (fixed)
- aml_target_gdc (2,614 samples, 39K genes): OOM → 71s (fixed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix e2e tests for null-correlation genes in co-expression response

Update co-expression e2e tests to account for genes with null
spearmansCorrelation and pValue (uncorrelatable genes: constant
expression, too few samples, or low variance). These genes are now
included in the response with null values.

- Filter correlated vs null-correlation genes before threshold assertions
- Add assertions verifying null-correlation genes have null pValue
- Remove sort-order assertion (endpoint does not guarantee order)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update src/main/resources/mappers/clickhouse/coexpression/ClickhouseCoExpressionMapper.xml

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

engine. All files include prominent legal disclaimers stating this tool

- requirements.txt Python dependencies

- core/loader.py      PayloadLoader (.bin/.raw binary, .hex/.txt hex-encoded)
- core/obfuscator.py  4-layer evasion orchestrator
- core/compiler.py    C/Rust/Go compilation via MinGW/rustc/go
- core/tester.py      Iterative obfuscate-test loop

Layer 1 – Static Evasion:
  encryption/  xor_encrypt, aes_encrypt (BCrypt), rc4_encrypt, chacha20_encrypt
